budget_wines.name: Nicolas Feuillatte Reserve Exclusive Brut NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Widely stocked in French supermarkets and wine shops; cooperative cellar at Chouilly and major retailers across France.
budget_wines.description: The benchmark cooperative non-vintage from France's biggest Champagne brand, drawing on a vast grower base. Creamy, apple-and-brioche style with several years on lees, the most reliable sub-EUR-35 fizz in the region.
budget_wines.tip: The easiest real-value entry point to Champagne; consistent year to year and almost always under EUR 35 in France.

budget_wines.name: Henriot Brut Souverain NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Henriot house in Reims and independent French wine merchants; broad distribution in France and export markets.
budget_wines.description: Chardonnay-led house brut from a historic Reims maison, all citrus peel, toasted brioche and chalky cut. Punches above its price for a grandes-marques non-vintage that often sits under EUR 45.
budget_wines.tip: A Chardonnay-leaning house style; serve it as an aperitif or with oysters for a classic Champagne match.

budget_wines.name: Pol Roger Brut Reserve NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Pol Roger house in Epernay and wine merchants worldwide; one of the most widely distributed family houses.
budget_wines.description: The equal-thirds non-vintage from one of Epernay's great family houses, famously Churchill's Champagne. Precise, fine-beaded and long, it is the value benchmark among the grandes marques when found near EUR 40.
budget_wines.tip: Look for it on offer around EUR 40; few grandes-marques brut NV deliver this much finesse at the price.

budget_wines.name: Chartogne-Taillet Cuvee Sainte-Anne Brut NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Chartogne-Taillet estate at Merfy and specialist grower-Champagne merchants in France and abroad.
budget_wines.description: The gateway grower cuvee from Alexandre Chartogne in the forgotten village of Merfy, near Reims. Chalky, savoury and detailed, it shows why grower fizz delivers real terroir value below EUR 45.
budget_wines.tip: The smartest grower-Champagne value here; buy the entry Sainte-Anne to taste Merfy before trading up to the single-vineyard cuvees.

budget_wines.name: Andre Clouet Grande Reserve Brut NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Andre Clouet estate at Bouzy and grower-focused merchants; broadly available in export markets.
budget_wines.description: A Grand Cru blanc de noirs from Bouzy, pure Pinot Noir grown and bottled by the same family. Rich, red-fruited and toasty, it offers Grand Cru fruit at a non-vintage price near EUR 40.
budget_wines.tip: All Bouzy Grand Cru Pinot Noir; a powerful, food-friendly grower style and a genuine value for the cru.

budget_wines.name: Pierre Gimonnet Cuis 1er Cru Brut Blanc de Blancs NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Pierre Gimonnet et Fils at Cuis in the Cote des Blancs and specialist merchants in France and abroad.
budget_wines.description: All-Chardonnay Premier Cru blanc de blancs from the Cote des Blancs grower Gimonnet. Linear, lemony and fine, a benchmark of chalk-driven Chardonnay Champagne for under EUR 45.
budget_wines.tip: A pure Cote des Blancs blanc de blancs at a non-vintage price; ideal as a crisp aperitif fizz.

budget_wines.name: Champagne Fleury Blanc de Noirs NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Champagne Fleury at Courteron in the Cote des Bar and organic and biodynamic wine merchants.
budget_wines.description: Biodynamic Pinot Noir blanc de noirs from Fleury, the first Demeter house in Champagne. Ripe red fruit and chalk over a low-intervention frame, real value for a certified biodynamic grower bottle.
budget_wines.tip: A certified biodynamic grower fizz; the value choice if you want green-farmed Champagne under EUR 50.

budget_wines.name: Lallier R.Series Grand Cru Brut NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Champagne Lallier at Ay and wine merchants in France and export markets.
budget_wines.description: Grand Cru-sourced non-vintage from the Ay house Lallier, dosed low and bottled with a disgorgement series number. Ripe, structured and chalky, a Grand Cru blend that lands near EUR 40.
budget_wines.tip: Check the R-number on the back label; it shows the disgorgement series for a fresher pour.

budget_wines.name: Georges Laval Cumieres Premier Cru Brut Nature NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Georges Laval at Cumieres and natural and organic wine specialists; small allocations.
budget_wines.description: Organic, zero-dosage Premier Cru from the tiny Laval domaine at Cumieres. Vinous, dry and mineral, it is the entry point to one of Champagne's great organic grower addresses, value despite the higher band.
budget_wines.tip: Allocations are tiny and prices have crept up; the entry Cumieres Brut Nature remains the way in to Laval.

budget_wines.name: Larmandier-Bernier Latitude Extra Brut Blanc de Blancs NV
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Larmandier-Bernier at Vertus in the Cote des Blancs and organic and grower merchants.
budget_wines.description: Organically farmed Cote des Blancs blanc de blancs from Larmandier-Bernier, sourced from southern Vertus chalk. Saline, taut and low-dosage, the value door into a benchmark organic grower house.
budget_wines.tip: Latitude is the village-level cuvee; start here before the Premier and Grand Cru single-village bottlings.
